props.title: Kerp On AI As Daily Workbench
props.subtitle: Proxy collapse, patient tutors, context infrastructure, and the new scarce work

cards.title: AI as daily workbench
cards.body: AI showed up as practical infrastructure across teaching, writing, startup judging, course tooling, product experiments, and context carryover.
cards.quote: The human still owns the judgment.

cards.title: Proxy collapse
cards.body: When AI weakens essays and reflection papers as proof of understanding, assessment has to move toward live explanation, interviews, oral exams, and follow-up probing.

cards.title: The patient tutor pattern
cards.body: Claude Code becomes more than a shortcut: it can explain libraries, schemas, security logic, and implementation decisions while the work is happening.

cards.title: Context as infrastructure
cards.body: Hypercontext points at the mess everyone recognizes: work spread across machines, chats, clients, cloud sync, style docs, and recurring project notes.

cards.title: The scarce work shifts
cards.body: As apps, decks, prototypes, and drafts get cheaper, the scarce work becomes taste, sequencing, QA, judgment, source inspection, and distribution.
